satire_attempt: The senior engineer, having internalised BG 2.68, opens a six-page Notion document titled 'attention-surfaces.md (revision 14)' and adds a new section: 'Restraint Policy: PagerDuty-tier-0-only-from-09:00-13:00-Wed-Thu (sarvaśaḥ).' She schedules a quarterly audit with herself. Her LinkedIn announces her new staff-engineer talk: 'How I Achieved Pratiṣṭhitā by Configuring DNS-Layer Blocks.' The audience claps. The DNS-layer block fails twice in production because she misconfigured the resolver. Her prajñā remains established, she reports, while she paged the on-call engineer to fix her own dotfiles.
verdict_reason: My satire trades on the analog's deliberate-architecture earnestness and on the LinkedIn-receivable form of 'I have configured my way to enlightenment'. But the verse's actual claim — that the state of established prajñā is structural (the configuration enables; willpower does not) and that the configuration must distinguish noise from signal — is not damaged by my mockery. The falsifiability clause ('the architecture restrains the noise; it does not restrain the signal') closes the misread my satire borrows from. The verse's stance is operationally falsifiable (does the engineer's reviewer-discovered-defect rate fall under the configured deep-work block vs. the unconfigured?). My satire mocks performance of the practice, not the practice itself; the verse remains intact. Pass.
